'''John Bateman''' (born 30 September 1993) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sr}} for the [[North Queensland Cowboys]] in the [[National Rugby League]], and [[England national rugby league team|England]] and [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] at international level.

He previously played for the [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]], [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] and [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] in the [[Super League]], and for the [[Canberra Raiders]] and [[Wests Tigers]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]]. Earlier in his career, he played as a {{rlp|ce}}.

==Background==
Bateman was born in [[Bradford]], West Yorkshire, England.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> He is a product of the [[Bradford Dudley Hill]] amateur Rugby League club.

==Club career==
===Bradford===
====2011 season====
In April 2011, at the age of 17 years and 208 days old Bateman made his [[Super League]] début starting at {{rlp|LK}} for the Bradford Bulls in an 8–8 draw against [[Catalans Dragons]].<ref>{{cite web|last1=Murphy|first1=Connor|title=Teenager Bateman gives encouraging début display for patched-up Bulls|url=http://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/news/8992595.display|website=Bradford Telegraph & Argus|access-date=9 December 2017|date=26 April 2011}}</ref> In May 2011, he made his first [[Challenge Cup]] appearance for the club against [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]]. He made two more first-team appearances towards the end of the season and scored his first Super League try against [[Wigan Warriors]].<ref>{{cite web|title=Wigan 60-12 Bradford|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/9566110.stm|website=BBC Sport|access-date=9 December 2017|date=19 August 2011}}</ref>

====2012 season====
Bateman featured in Round 1 ([[Catalans Dragons]]). He missed Rounds 2–3 due to injury. He featured in six consecutive games from Round 4 ([[Wakefield Trinity]]) to Round 9 ([[Hull F.C.]]). Bateman was suspended for rounds 10 and 11. John returned in Round 12 ([[Huddersfield Giants]]). Bateman was suspended for Rounds 13–14. He returned to play in six consecutive games from Round 15 ([[Leeds Rhinos]]) to Round 20 ([[London Broncos]]). Bateman missed Rounds 21–24 due to England Academy commitments. Bateman returned for Round 25 ([[Huddersfield Giants]]). John was injured in Rounds 26–27. He featured in the [[Challenge Cup]] against [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]] and [[Warrington Wolves]]. Bateman scored against [[Hull F.C.]] (1 try), [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]] (1 try) and [[Castleford Tigers]] (1 try).

In 2012 Bateman was awarded the [[Albert Goldthorpe Medal|Albert Goldthorpe Rookie of the Year Medal]].<ref>{{cite web|title=The Albert Goldthorpe Awards|url=http://www.totalrl.com/web/the-albert-goldthorpe-awards-roll-of-honour|work=totalrl.com|publisher=League Publications|access-date=16 February 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140326074057/http://www.totalrl.com/web/the-albert-goldthorpe-awards-roll-of-honour/|archive-date=26 March 2014|url-status=dead}}</ref>

====2013 season====
In the off-season, Bateman signed a 3 Year Extension on his current deal and this will keep him at Odsal until 2015.

He missed Rounds 1–6 due to an injury. He featured in four consecutive games from Round 7 ([[Hull Kingston Rovers]]) to Round 10 ([[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]]). John missed Round 11–12 due to an injury. He featured in Round 13 ([[Warrington Wolves]]) but was subject to a groin injury keeping him out of Round 14. John returned for Round 15 ([[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]]) but missed Round 16 but returned in Round 17 ([[Hull Kingston Rovers]]) to Round 22 ([[Hull F.C.]]). John missed the rest of the season due to surgery on his ankle. Bateman featured in the [[Challenge Cup]] against [[Rochdale Hornets]] and [[London Broncos]]. John scored against [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] (1 try), [[Catalans Dragons]] (1 try) [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] (1 try), [[Rochdale Hornets]] (1 try) and [[Wigan Warriors]] (1 try).{{cn|date=October 2022}}

===Wigan===
In November 2013, Bateman signed a three-year contract with [[Wigan Warriors]] for an undisclosed fee,<ref>{{cite web|title=Wigan sign the Bradford Bulls back-rower John Bateman|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/24773355|website=BBC Sport|access-date=9 December 2017|date=1 November 2013}}</ref> believed to be around £70,000.<ref>{{cite web|last1=Heppenstall|first1=Ross|title=John Bateman big interview: I bit their hands off when Wigan came calling|url=http://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/11160570.John_Bateman_big_interview__I_bit_their_hands_off_when_Wigan_came_calling|website=Bradford Telegraph and Argus|access-date=9 December 2017|date=21 April 2014}}</ref>

====2014====
Bateman featured in the [[World Club Challenge]] against [[Sydney Roosters]]. He featured in Round 2 ([[Castleford Tigers]]) to Round 15 ([[Huddersfield Giants]]) then in Round 18 ([[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]]) to Round 23 ([[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]]). John next played in Round 25 ([[Widnes Vikings]]) to Round 27 ([[Warrington Wolves]]). Bateman played in Round 5 ([[Hunslet R.L.F.C.|Hunslet]]) to the Quarter Final ([[Castleford Tigers]]) in the [[Challenge Cup]]. John played in the Qualifying Playoff ([[Huddersfield Giants]]). He scored against [[Wakefield Trinity]] (1 try), the [[Bradford Bulls]] (1 try), [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] (1 try) and [[London Broncos]] (1 try).{{cn|date=October 2022}}

====2015====
Bateman played in the [[World Club Series]] against [[Brisbane Broncos]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=2015-02-18 |title=Wigan preparing without O'Loughlin |url=https://www.eurosport.com/rugby-league/wigan-preparing-without-o-loughlin_sto4607708/story.shtml |access-date=2023-12-20 |website=Eurosport |language=en}}</ref> 
[[File:John Bateman 2015.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Bateman playing for Wigan in 2015]]
Bateman featured in Round 1 ([[Widnes Vikings]]) to Round 9 ([[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]]) then in Round 11 ([[Warrington Wolves]]) to Round 19 ([[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]]). He also played in Round 21 ([[Leeds Rhinos]]) to Super Eight 6 ([[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]]) then in the Semi Final ([[Huddersfield Giants]]) to the Grand Final ([[Leeds Rhinos]]). Bateman played in the [[Challenge Cup]] in Round 6 ([[Hull Kingston Rovers]]). He scored against [[Huddersfield Giants]] (4 tries), [[Hull F.C.]] (2 tries), [[Leeds Rhinos]] (1 try) and [[Warrington Wolves]] (2 tries).

He played in the [[2015 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by the [[Leeds Rhinos]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="Leeds pip Wigan to seal treble after brilliant, breathless Grand Final">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2015/oct/10/leeds-wigan-super-league-grand-final-match-report|title=Leeds pip Wigan to seal treble after brilliant, breathless Grand Final|publisher=Guardian|date=10 October 2015|access-date=8 November 2019}}</ref>

====2016====
Bateman featured in Round 1 ([[Catalans Dragons]]) to Round 8 ([[Hull Kingston Rovers]]) then in Round 11 ([[Castleford Tigers]]) to Round 12 ([[Huddersfield Giants]]). He featured in the World Club Series against [[Brisbane Broncos]]. He scored against [[Hull F.C.]] (1 try), [[Leeds Rhinos]] (1 try), [[Castleford Tigers]] (2 tries) and [[Huddersfield Giants]] (1 try). On 31 May 2016, Wigan confirmed they were investigating an "alleged fracas" between two Wigan players which took place outside of a gym on 27 May,<ref>{{cite web|title=Wigan Warriors Super League club probe gym 'fracas'|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/uk-england-manchester-36419595|website=BBC News|access-date=9 December 2017|date=31 May 2016}}</ref> and speculation grew that Bateman was linked to the controversy following his omission from the squad against [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] and [[Hull Kingston Rovers]].<ref>{{cite web|last1=Wilkinson|first1=Phil|title=Bateman situation "still the same"|url=https://www.wigantoday.net/sport/rugby-league/wigan-warriors/bateman-situation-still-the-same-1-7951275|website=Wigan Today|access-date=9 December 2017|date=7 June 2016}}</ref> On 15 June 2016, Bateman was fined £10,000 and suspended for eight weeks for his involvement in the incident.<ref>{{cite web|title=John Bateman: Wigan Warriors suspend England forward for alleged fracas|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/36539384|website=BBC Sport|access-date=9 December 2017|date=15 June 2016}}</ref>

He played in the [[2016 Super League Grand Final]] victory over the [[Warrington Wolves]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="Warrington 6-12 Wigan: Super League Grand Final – as it happened!">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2016/oct/08/super-league-grand-final-warrington-wigan-live|title=Warrington 6-12 Wigan: Super League Grand Final – as it happened!|publisher=Guardian|date=8 October 2016|access-date=6 November 2019}}</ref>

====2017====
He played in the [[2017 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by [[Hull F.C.]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Hull FC 18-14 Wigan recap as the Black and Whites claim back-to-back Challenge Cup triumphs">{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/challenge-cup-final-live-score-11054626|title=Hull FC 18-14 Wigan recap as the Black and Whites claim back-to-back Challenge Cup triumphs|publisher=Mirror|date=26 August 2017|access-date=6 November 2019}}</ref>

====2018====
[[File:Wane, Bateman & Goulding.jpg|thumb|right|[[Shaun Wane]], Bateman and [[Darrell Goulding]] at a Wigan match in 2018]]
He played in the [[2018 Super League Grand Final]] victory over the [[Warrington Wolves]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="Super League Grand Final 2018: Wigan 12-4 Warrington – as it happened">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2018/oct/13/super-league-grand-final-2018-wigan-warriors-v-warrington-wolves-live?page=with:block-5bc1ff43e4b05798be94cc63#liveblog-navigation|title=Super League Grand Final 2018: Wigan 12-4 Warrington – as it happened|publisher=Guardian|date=13 October 2018|access-date=5 November 2019}}</ref>

===Canberra===
====2019====
Bateman signed with the [[Canberra Raiders]] for 2019 and 2020, with a third-year option in his favour, on 8 August 2018. In round 19 of the [[2019 NRL season]] against the [[Penrith Panthers]], Bateman scored a try in the dying minutes to seal a 30-18 victory for the visitors away from home. The win cemented Canberra's position in the top 4 of the competition.

At the end of the 2019 regular season, Canberra finished 4th on the table and qualified for the finals.  In the qualifying final against Melbourne, Bateman scored the winning try with just 3 minutes remaining. Canberra staged an upset victory at [[AAMI Park]] winning 12-10 and securing a home preliminary final.  In the preliminary final against [[South Sydney Rabbitohs|South Sydney]], Bateman played at second-row as Canberra won the match 16-10 at [[Canberra Stadium]] and qualified for their first grand final in 25 years.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2019/sep/27/canberra-raiders-end-25-year-wait-to-reach-nrl-grand-final-with-victory-over-rabbitohs/|title=Canberra Raiders end 25-year wait to reach NRL grand final with victory over Rabbitohs|website=The Guardian|date=27 September 2019 }}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2019/sep/14/raiders-upset-storm-to-advance-to-nrl-preliminary-finals/|title=Raiders upset Storm to advance to NRL preliminary final|website=The Guardian|date=14 September 2019 }}</ref> On 2 October, Bateman was named as the 2019 Dally M second-rower of the year at the [[Dally M Awards]] ceremony.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2019/10/02/dally-m-awards-2019-best-of-the-best/|title=Tedesco tops list of 2019 Dally M award winners|website=NRL|date=2 October 2019 }}</ref>

Bateman played at {{rlp|sr}} for Canberra in the [[2019 NRL Grand Final]] as they were defeated 14-8 by the [[Sydney Roosters]] at [[ANZ Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/nrl-grand-final-player-ratings-roosters-and-raiders-hits-and-misses-20191006-p52y53.html/|title=NRL grand final player ratings: Roosters and Raiders hits and misses|website=SMH|date=6 October 2019 }}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/49950757|title=Sydney Roosters beat Canberra Raiders to win NRL Grand Final|work=BBC Sport |date=6 October 2019 }}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/grand-final-ricky-stuart-reaction-canberra-raiders-six-again-jack-wighton/ae39b639-d375-4608-89a6-af6285c3d635/|title=How Ricky Stuart reacted to grand final 'six-again' refereeing shocker|website=WWOS}}</ref>

====2020====
In February, it was revealed that Bateman would miss the first six weeks of the [[2020 NRL season]] after undergoing shoulder surgery.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/john-bateman-to-miss-first-six-weeks-of-nrl-season/|title=John Bateman to miss first six weeks of NRL season|website=www.loverugbyleague.com|date=27 February 2020 }}</ref>
On 31 June, it was announced that he would be departing the [[Canberra Raiders]] at the end of the season, With [[NRL]] Club, the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]] And His former side, The [[Wigan Warriors]] interested in his services.

Bateman played 11 games for Canberra in the [[2020 NRL season]]. He featured in all three of Canberra's finals matches, including Melbourne's preliminary final loss.  After the game had concluded, a visibly emotional Bateman thanked everyone at Canberra and stated it would be hard to leave the club.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/nrl-news-john-bateman-canberra-raiders/60f92958-91d6-4bee-8e00-ed0cac9a2160/|title=Departing Bateman overcome with emotion after season-ending loss to Melbourne|website=wwos.nine.com.au}}</ref>

==== 2021 ====
In August 2021, it was revealed that Bateman had been involved in a fistfight with then-Canberra player [[Curtis Scott]].  The incident was not reported to
the police and the matter was sorted behind closed doors but the story came to light after Scott's termination by the Canberra club.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/raiders/nrl-news-curtis-scott-canberra-raiders-john-bateman-fight-sacked-contract-career/news-story/6d8a1b65aed1429fd735ab4467d89dae/|title=The secret fight with teammate that led to Raiders sacking Curtis Scott|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=23 August 2021 }}</ref>

===Re-Joining Wigan===
In July 2020, it was announced that Bateman would rejoin [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] for the 2021 season.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/53285056|title=John Bateman: Wigan Warriors re-sign England forward from Canberra Raiders from 2021|date=3 July 2020 |publisher=BBC Sport|access-date=10 Dec 2020}}</ref>

==== 2021 ====
In round 20 of the [[2021 Super League season]], Bateman was sent to the sin bin twice in Wigan's 26-2 loss against St Helens.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/58287751|title=Super League: Wigan Warriors 2-26 St Helens - Saints coast to highly charged derby win|work=BBC Sport |date=20 August 2021 }}</ref>

==== 2022 ====
In the 2022 semi-final, Bateman was sent off for a dangerous high tackle on former teammate [[Aiden Sezer]] during the club's shock defeat to [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2022/sep/16/leeds-pull-out-stunning-win-at-wigan-to-reach-grand-final/|title=Leeds stun Wigan to reach Grand Final as remarkable resurgence goes on|newspaper=The Guardian|date=16 September 2022 |last1=Bower |first1=Aaron }}</ref>

=== Wests Tigers ===
On 26 December, Bateman left [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] to sign a four-year deal with the [[National Rugby League|NRL]] club [[Wests Tigers]] for 2023.<ref>{{Cite web |title=John Bateman set for NRL return after signing a four-year deal with Wests Tigers |url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/rugby-league/news/john-bateman-set-nrl-return-after-signing-four-year-deal-wests-tigers/icwj3qk8tcvagjckughvqirk |access-date=2022-12-26 |website=www.sportingnews.com |date=26 December 2022 |language=en}}</ref>
In round 3 of the [[2023 NRL season]], Bateman made his long-awaited club debut for the Wests Tigers in their 26-22 loss against [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs|Canterbury]] at [[Belmore Sports Ground]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-live-canterburybankstown-bulldogs-vs-wests-tigers-live-scores-team-lists-supercoach-live-news-john-bateman-debut-highlights/news-story/08918aee00441684f576f470cbc799fc/|title=Blowtorch on the Tigers as worst nightmare emerges; 'tremendous' Dogs young gun: Big Hits|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=19 March 2023 }}</ref> He played in 20 games, leading Wests Tigers in offloads, with the 14th most in the NRL. He also led the club in tackles made.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/stats/players/?competition=111&season=2023&stat=28|title=Offloads|website=nrl.com}}</ref>
The club would finish bottom of the table in 2023 and claim their second [[Wooden Spoon (award)|Wooden Spoon]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2023-wests-tigers-season-in-review-tigers-brutal-review-tim-sheens-benji-marshall-coach-justin-pascoe-wooden-spoon/news-story/d1ae7fb41d93ea7dc29173a824cac5a1/|title='Circus act' that summed up year from hell... and big call left for Benji: Wests Tigers Brutal Review|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=5 September 2023 }}</ref>
On 14 May 2024, it was announced that Bateman would miss up to 8-10 weeks with a pectoral injury which he suffered during the clubs narrow loss to [[Newcastle Knights|Newcastle]] in round 10 of the [[2024 NRL season]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2023-wests-tigers-season-in-review-tigers-brutal-review-tim-sheens-benji-marshall-coach-justin-pascoe-wooden-spoon/news-story/d1ae7fb41d93ea7dc29173a824cac5a1/|title='Circus act' that summed up year from hell... and big call left for Benji: Wests Tigers Brutal Review|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=5 September 2023 }}</ref> On 23 July 2024, Bateman joined the [[Warrington Wolves]] on loan for the remainder of the 2024 season.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/warrington-wolves-complete-john-bateman-29592877 | title=Warrington Wolves complete John Bateman coup as England star makes Super League return | website=Yorkshire Live | date=23 July 2024}}</ref>

====Warrington Wolves (loan)====
On 24 July 2024 it was reported that he had signed for [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] in the [[Super League]] on season-long loan.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=24 Jul 2024|author=Aaron Bower|title=Warrington Wolves’ all-star pack options analysed after John Bateman deal|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/warrington-wolves-all-star-pack-options-analysed-after-john-bateman-deal}}</ref>

===North Queensland Cowboys===
On 18 December 2024, Bateman was released by the Wests Tigers, signing a two-year contract with the [[North Queensland Cowboys]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.cowboys.com.au/news/2024/12/18/bateman-joins-cowboys-2526-1/|title=Bateman joins Cowboys|date=18 December 2024|website=NQ Cowboys}}</ref>
Bateman played 19 matches for North Queensland in the [[2025 NRL season]] as the club finished 12th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nine.com.au/sport/nrl/news-2025-the-mole-season-review-north-queensland-cowboys-jason-taumalolo-deal-20250911-p5mugv.html/|title=The Mole's season review: 'Leaderless' Cowboys prove $10m gamble on Jason Taumalolo was a flop|website=www.nine.com.au}}</ref>

==International career==
[[File:John Bateman England.jpg|thumb|right|Bateman warming up for England in 2016]]

In 2012, Bateman was named captain of the England Academy squad to tour Australia.

Bateman made his [[England national rugby league team|England]] test début against France in October 2015.

After an outstanding Super League 2015 season, Bateman was selected in the senior [[England national rugby league team|England team]] for their [[2015 New Zealand rugby league tour of Great Britain|test series against New Zealand]]. Bateman made his début for England in a [http://www.sportinglife.com/rugby-league/live/match-report/43791/england-v-france test match] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20151026223631/http://www.sportinglife.com/rugby-league/live/match-report/43791/england-v-france |date=26 October 2015 }}, before the series, against [[France national rugby league team|France]] which saw Bateman score two tries in England's rout of their opponents. For his outstanding efforts in the game, Bateman was awarded man-of-the-match.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/live/match/43791/report|title=England demolish France 84–4 in record win|publisher=Skysports.com|access-date=24 October 2015}}</ref>

In October 2016, Bateman was selected in the England 24-man squad for the [[2016 Rugby League Four Nations|2016 Four Nations]].

In October 2017 he was selected in the [[2017_Rugby_League_World_Cup_squads#England|England squad]] for the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="England rugby league World Cup squad revealed - and James Roby is back in the fold">{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/james-roby-back-fold-wayne-11312505|title=England rugby league World Cup squad revealed - and James Roby is back in the fold|publisher=Mirror|date=9 October 2017|access-date=30 October 2019}}</ref>

He was selected in squad for the [[2019 Great Britain Lions tour]] of the Southern Hemisphere.<ref name="Zak Hardaker shock inclusion in 24-man Great Britain squad for tour of New Zealand and Papua New Guinea">{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/rugby-league/2019/10/14/zak-hardaker-shock-inclusion-24-man-great-britain-squad-tour/|title=Zak Hardaker shock inclusion in 24-man Great Britain squad for tour of New Zealand and Papua New Guinea|publisher=Telegraph|date=14 October 2019|access-date=29 October 2019}}</ref> He made his [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] test debut in the defeat by Tonga.<ref name="Great Britain left bruised at Tonga party">{{cite web|url=https://www.thetimes.com/article/great-britain-tonga-rugby-league-hzmmhr5q5|title=Great Britain left bruised at Tonga party|publisher=Times|date=26 October 2019|access-date=29 October 2019}}</ref>
